title: Retraction Notice: Home: IOT based home automation using NFC
authors: P. Vagdevi, Divya Nagaraj, Golla Vara Prasad
publish: 2017 International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: One of the emerging technologies in today's World having spread its usefulness in a wide variety of domains is near-field communication. From automation of student's attendance monitoring to the ticketing system, it has been widely accepted across many countries. Its main feature lies in automation like automating car for locking/unlocking car doors, switching in GPS, turning on radio/speakers etc. The internet of things has given us opportunity to automate our home appliance and control it from a certain distance. A home automation system has become a trend, which gives a convenient way for users to personalize artifacts in home using sensors, Bluetooth and android system. This paper proposes the architecture for home automations using near field communication and general packet radio service along with a mobile application. The near-field communication is used as the central system in this paper to automate home environment for a user by locking and unlocking doors, lighting, air conditioning, switching on/off fans, personal computer, mobile, television as near field communication helps in providing energy efficiency and required security.

title: Offloading Post-Quantum Cryptography to Data Processing Units: A Performance Analysis of HQC Key Encapsulation
authors: Joseph Meyer, Elad Mentovich, Idelfonso Tafur Monroy, J. J. Vegas Olmos
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The transition to post-quantum cryptography introduces substantial computational overhead that can degrade application performance in datacenter environments, particularly under system contention when there is competition to access shared computational resources. This work presents the first experimental evaluation of offloading Hamming Quasi-Cyclic (HQC) key encapsulation—a code-based algorithm advancing through the fourth standardization round of the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)—to high-capacity Data Processing Units (DPUs). We systematically compare host-based execution on x86 processors against DPU-offloaded execution on NVIDIA BlueField-3 400Gbit/s-capable devices across three security levels and four server load regimes, evaluating both single-threaded and multi-threaded workloads. Under idle conditions, host-based execution achieves approximately 22% lower latency than DPU execution due to micro-architectural advantages. However, under moderate to heavy server contention, DPU-offloaded execution achieves 31–34% lower latency, with the advantage increasing at higher security levels. Under multi-threaded operation, DPU throughput scales near-linearly and exceeds host throughput by up to 2.9× under idle conditions and 5.5× under heavy load. Beyond absolute performance, DPU offloading dramatically improves execution determinism: host-based latency variance exceeds 34% under heavy load, while DPU execution maintains variance below 0.1% regardless of host activity or DPU-local background traffic. These results demonstrate that architectural isolation provided by DPUs enables predictable post-quantum cryptographic performance in datacenter environments where system contention is unavoidable—a finding supported by cross-platform validation, analytical latency decomposition, and first-principles analysis of the underlying isolation mechanism.

title: Compound Neural Network based Nonsingular Fixed-Time SMC for Multi-Unit Distributed Permanent Magnet Arc Motor Servo Systems
authors: Qiangren Xu, Shuhua Fang, Xiangru Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The article proposes a compound neural network based nonsingular fixed-time SMC (CN3FxTSMC) for multi-unit distributed permanent magnet arc motor (PMAM) servo systems. In PMAMs, Multi-source periodic disturbances dominated by end torque are mitigated by a novel neural architecture that emphasizes short-term memory and periodicity feature while preserving minimal input dimensions. Position-tracking error is supplied as the only external input to decouple training from velocity-measurement noise. A nonsingular fixed-time sliding-mode controller is employed together with a newly designed fixed-time weight-adaptation law to accelerate network learning. To accommodate abrupt, high-magnitude torque transients that displace the preset centers of the wavelet functions and impair tracking of nonlinear time-varying disturbances, a high-order sliding-mode disturbance observer is integrated to compensate external perturbations and retain the network within its optimal operating range. The proposed CN3FxTSMC can provide a comprehensive enhancement for the convergence accuracy, convergence rate, and applicability of the neural network. Lyapunov theorem verifies the stability of the system and fixed-time convergence. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the CN<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>FxTSMC.

title: Stator Current Control Strategy of High-Speed Maglev Train to Simultaneously Suppress Aperiodic and Periodic Disturbances
authors: Hongyun Sang, Ruihua Zhang, Jinquan Zhu, Lu Zhao, Qiongxuan Ge
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: To simultaneously suppress aperiodic and periodic disturbances in the current loop of the long-stator linear synchronous motor (LS-LSM) for high-speed maglev trains, this article proposes a composite stator current control strategy integrating a non-smooth controller (NSC) and a generalized integrator disturbance observer (GIDO). The NSC is proposed to suppress aperiodic disturbances, with finite-time convergence proven and a practical parameter tuning method provided. A GIDO-based feedforward loop is proposed for periodic disturbances suppression, which eliminates resonant magnitude peak accompanied by abrupt phase changes to enhance dynamic performance and enables the separation of positive- and negative-sequence disturbance components. A delay compensation strategy is proposed to guarantee system stability under low-carrier-ratio conditions. The effectiveness of the proposed control strategy is verified on a hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) simulation platform and an electrically excited synchronous motor platform.

title: A Coordinated Control Strategy with a Variable Intermediate Bus Voltage for Efficiency Optimization in Two-Stage Buck-DAB Converters
authors: Zhuyu Shen, Zhengmei Lu, Pengfeng Lin, Miao Zhu, Wenjie Bi, Guo Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: In conventional two-stage dual-active-bridge (DAB)- based converters, the intermediate bus voltage is fixed to maintain the unity voltage gain of the DAB for wide-range soft switching and high efficiency. Nevertheless, such a simplified design neglects the global efficiency characteristics of the system and cannot ensure optimal operation. To address this limitation, this article proposes an efficiency optimization scheme with a variable intermediate bus voltage using particle swarm optimization (PSO) to improve overall system efficiency. The soft-switching and loss model of the Buck-DAB converter is established in this paper, indicating that the system efficiency of the two-stage converter is a function of the intermediate bus voltage. Based on this, efficiency optimization can be achieved by adjusting the bus voltage while maintaining soft-switching operation. The proposed method determines the optimal intermediate bus voltage using an offline PSO algorithm, thereby achieving soft-switching operation and efficiency improvement of the converter over a wide operating range. Finally, a 0.7 KW prototyped is built, and the experimental results demonstrate that the power efficiency can be improved by using the optimization scheme based on the PSO.

title:
PDP-PINN: A Fourier-Augmented Physics-Informed Neural Network for Pressure Drop Prediction in CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -Injected Porous Media
authors: Nafisa Binte Ghulam Kibria, Amith Khandakar, Md. Faysal Ahamed, Hafsa Binte Kibria, Mohammad Azizur Rahman, Mohamed Arselene Ayari
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Accurate prediction of pressure variations during CO2 injection is crucial for the safe and efficient operation of carbon capture and storage (CCS) in subsurface geological formations. Excessive pressure can compromise caprock integrity or create leakage pathways, posing significant operational risks. Traditional numerical simulations, while precise, are computationally intensive and often unsuitable for real-time applications. This study presents PDP-PINN, a Fourier-augmented physics-informed neural network designed for pressure drop prediction across heterogeneous porous media during CO2 injection. The model integrates spatio-temporal flow features with physical constraints derived from Darcy’s law and employs Fourier feature embeddings to mitigate spectral bias. Evaluated on a high-resolution two-phase CO2-water simulation dataset, PDP-PINN achieves strong predictive performance across various heterogeneity levels, with a coefficient of determination (R2) up to 0.9941 and mean squared error (MSE) of 5.56, while maintaining physical consistency. Furthermore, explainable AI techniques, including SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) and Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME), are applied to provide global and local interpretability, revealing the influence of individual features on model predictions. The proposed architecture demonstrates potential for real-time injectivity assessment, flow resistance estimation, and subsurface risk mitigation in CCS operations.

title: Cross-Corpus Speech Emotion Recognition Based on Dynamically Filtering Multi-Stage Diffusion Model
authors: Jingjie Yan, Boyan Sun, Yuebo Yue, Xiaoyang Zhou, Ying Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: In cross-corpus speech emotion recognition (SER) tasks, although generative methods have shown certain potential in alleviating the scarcity of target domain samples, the generated samples often lack precise alignment with the acousticsemantic features of the target domain. This results in pseudo samples of uneven quality and even introduces noise, thereby weakening the effectiveness of transfer learning. To address the problem of insufficient quality control over pseudo samples in existing methods, we propose a Dynamically Filtering Multi- Stage Diffusion Model (DFMDM). The proposed DFMDM model establishes a three-stage iterative optimization process of “Generation– Filtering–Enhancement.” In the first stage, the conditional diffusion model is guided by an adversarial domain classifier to generate pseudo samples with target domain characteristics. In the second stage, a dynamic filtering module is presented to eliminate low-quality pseudo samples that significantly deviate from the target domain features. In the third stage, the filtered pseudo samples are mixed with source domain samples and reused to regenerate target-style pseudo samples. In this way, a progressive diffusion-based enhancement loop is constructed, which improves sample quality through iterative generation and dynamic adjustment of similarity thresholds. Experimental results conducted on CASIA, EmoDB, eNTERFACE and IEMOCAP demonstrate that the proposed DFMDM model achieves significant improvements over existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) approaches, fully showcasing its application potential and practical value in cross-corpus speech emotion recognition.

title: GNSS Multi-Frequency Joint Code Tracking Loop With Dynamic Inter-Frequency Bias Estimation and Compensation
authors: Jing Zhou, Zuping Tang, Rong Yang, Jiaolong Wei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: The joint reception of global navigation satellite system (GNSS) multi-frequency signals can enhance loop tracking accuracy and robustness, particularly in challenging environments. However, dynamic inter-frequency bias (IFB) exists between the code delays of different frequency bands, disrupting coherence and degrading tracking performance. Existing methods typically treat IFB as prior information for compensation, restricting the adaptability and practical deployment of multi-frequency reception technologies. This paper proposes a multi-frequency joint code tracking loop architecture that tracks and compensates for dynamic IFB, without relying on ionospheric delay models or other prior information. The proposed method is fully compatible with standard tracking architectures, enabling seamless integration into existing GNSS receivers. To validate its effectiveness, theoretical analyses are conducted, including thermal noise jitter calculation formula and dynamic stress response characteristics. Monte Carlo simulations are performed using Global Positioning System (GPS) L1 C/A and L5 signals to validate the theoretical model. Real-world data experiments confirm that the proposed loop significantly enhances L1 positioning accuracy in urban environments, achieving up to 78.4% improvement over the standard tracking loop (STL).

title: Privacy Preserved and Explainable Deep Medical Image Analysis: A Survey
authors: Linkon Chowdhury, Selvarajah Thuseethan, Yakub Sebastian, Sutharshan Rajasegarar, Mamoun Alazab, John Yearwood
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Deep neural networks play a significant role in medical image analysis, particularly in improving the efficiency and accuracy of disease diagnosis and treatment planning. The ability to preserve the privacy of medical data opens the door to harnessing more information to train powerful and intelligent AI models. However, the added complexity introduced by incorporating privacy measures often leads to increasingly opaque deep learning models. As the need to interpret these privacy-preserving deep learning models grows, explainable AI systems have become pivotal for cultivating trust among clinical experts and stakeholders. To address this challenge, researchers have begun to focus on developing privacy-preserving techniques with improved explainability features. This article presents a comprehensive survey of different privacy models and security techniques, focusing on biomedical imaging applications. The survey covers peer-reviewed studies published during 2019–2024, ensuring comprehensive and contemporary coverage of privacy-preserving and explainability techniques. It covers a detailed review of various privacy-preserving methods and studies related to model explanations. It also highlights unresolved challenges and suggests potential research directions. This survey aims to offer valuable directions to the research community by explaining privacy-preserving techniques for medical image analysis.

title: In-situ temperature-compensated puncture force sensing for brain stereotactic puncture needle using fiber Bragg grating
authors: Yanding Qin, Hongzhe Guo, Zikang Wen, Longxin Wang, Liang Yi, Yanqi Huang, Ge Fang, Hongpeng Wang, Jianda Han
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: In brain stereotactic surgery, real-time and accurate perception of the interaction force between the puncture needle and brain tissue is crucial for enhancing surgical precision and ensuring safety. This capability is essential for identifying different tissue interfaces and providing timely feedback to avoid damage or injury to delicate neural structures during the procedure. Fiber Bragg grating (FBG) sensor is attractive due to its precision, biocompatibility, and anti-interference performances. However, the inherent strain-temperature cross-sensitivity of FBG sensors degrades measurement accuracy. Conventional dual-grating techniques fail due to temperature gradients caused by spatial separation of gratings, resulting in measurement errors. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a novel in-situ temperature compensation method using a coplanar integrated reference grating. By placing gratings in the same location, this design fundamentally eliminates transient errors caused by spatial temperature gradients during needle insertion. The designs of 3-axis and 1-axis force sensors are presented. The puncture needle fabricated with this method exhibits excellent linearity within a 0-5 N measurement range. Furthermore, comparative puncture experiments are conducted in both water and agar phantom to test the in-situ temperature compensation FBG against a traditional dual-grating FBG. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed in-situ temperature compensation FBG sensor offers a significant advantage in resolving the temperature gradient problem encountered during the puncture process. In vivo animal experiments are conducted to validate the developed puncture needle, confirming the effectiveness of the integrated sensor.

title: LifelongPR: Lifelong Point Cloud Place Recognition Based on Sample Replay and Prompt Learning
authors: Xianghong Zou, Jianping Li, Zhe Chen, Zhen Cao, Zhen Dong, Qiegen Liu, Bisheng Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Point cloud place recognition (PCPR) determines the geo-location within a prebuilt map and plays a crucial role in photogrammetry and robotics applications such as autonomous driving, intelligent transportation, and augmented reality. In real-world large-scale deployments of a geographic positioning system, PCPR models must continuously acquire, update, and accumulate knowledge to adapt to diverse and dynamic environments, i.e., the ability known as continual learning (CL). However, existing PCPR models often suffer from catastrophic forgetting, leading to significant performance degradation in previously learned scenes when adapting to new environments or sensor types. This results in poor model scalability, increased maintenance costs, and system deployment difficulties, undermining the practicality of PCPR. To address these issues, we propose LifelongPR, a novel continual learning framework for PCPR, which effectively extracts and fuses knowledge from sequential point cloud data. First, to alleviate the knowledge loss, we propose a replay sample selection method that dynamically allocates sample sizes according to each dataset’s information quantity and selects spatially diverse samples for maximal representativeness. Second, to handle domain shifts, we design a prompt learning-based CL framework with a lightweight continuous prompt module and a two-stage training strategy, enabling domain-specific feature adaptation while minimizing forgetting. Comprehensive experiments on large-scale public and self-collected datasets are conducted to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. Compared with the state-of-the-art (SOTA) method, our method achieves 6.50% improvement in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$mIR\text{@}1$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, 7.96% improvement in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$mR\text{@}1$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, and an 8.95% reduction in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$F$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. The code and pre-trained models are publicly available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://zouxianghong.github.io/LifelongPR</uri>

title: The Shape of Emotion in Speech: A Topological Data Analysis of Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Michel B. Raskin
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Speech emotion recognition (SER) systems commonly use time-frequency representations of speech to predict emotions. While recent advances in SER have achieved strong classification performance, the underlying time-frequency representations provide limited interpretability. In thiswork, we create a novel framework for SER using topological data analysis (TDA) with a focus on interpretability. We accomplish this by constructing features from persistence images generated from three distance metrics: Euclidean, time-weighted Euclidean, and Wasserstein distances. Then, we evaluate four models: (i) CRNN, (ii) ResNet18, (iii) an MLP on persistence images, and (iv) a fusion model combining spectral and topological features. All experiments were done on four commonly used SER datasets: RAVDESS, CREMA-D, SAVEE, and TESS, with speaker-independent training and test splits to ensure generalization, and cross-corpus testing. Results show that topological features alone are capable of non-trivial classification performance (0.7900± 0.0064 AUROC and 0.4348±0.0130 accuracy on MLP). Moreover, the fusion model had the highest pooled speaker-independent performance with AUROC of 0.8774±0.0118, and accuracy of 0.6361±0.0261 with gains primarily driven by TESS. Under cross-dataset evaluation, performance decreases substantially for all methods, with ResNet18 providing the strongest overall generalization. Finally, ablations and integrated gradients indicate that the most important features come from the time-weighted Euclidean metric, and H0, suggesting that the temporal organization of speech is important. In summary, this demonstrates that TDA can provide interpretable insight into SER, and can complement standard spectrogram models.

title: Ultra-Wideband Angularly Stable Reflective Frequency Selective Surface for Millimeter-Wave Applications
authors: Sandhya Patel, Mohamed Mamdouh M. Ali, AbdelRazik Sebak
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents an ultra-wideband single-layer reflective frequency selective surface (FSS) for electromagnetic shielding and electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) in millimeter-wave systems. The proposed FSS exhibits a wide stopband covering the 5G millimeter-wave band from 24 GHz to 48 GHz, with a fractional bandwidth of approximately 65% under normal incidence. Its performance remains stable for oblique incidence angles from 0° to 75°, maintaining strong reflection for both TE and TM polarizations to effectively suppress unwanted electromagnetic interference (EMI). The compact, mirror-symmetric unit-cell design enables bidirectional operation, high angular stability, and polarization insensitivity, making it suitable for EMC-critical environments. An equivalent circuit model (ECM) analysis is conducted to provide physical insight into the FSS unit cell, clarifying the transmission-blocking mechanism and supporting the proposed design methodology. The simulation and measured results show good agreement, demonstrating that the proposed FSS is a promising candidate for mitigating Ka-band EMI and ensuring EMCin millimeter-wave applications.

title: Joint Optimization of Gateway Placement, Resource Configuration, and Uplink Scheduling for LoRaWAN Bridge Health Monitoring
authors: Avilia Kusumaputeri Nugroho, Donghee Yun, Jenn-Wei Lin, Taewoon Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a unified optimization framework for planning and configuring large-scale LoRaWAN deployments in linear bridge monitoring systems. The framework addresses key challenges arising from dense sensor deployment, single-channel gateway operation, and stringent regulatory constraints. In contrast to conventional approaches that optimize network components sequentially, the proposed framework jointly determines gateway placement, node assignment, physical-layer parameters, and uplink scheduling through a four-stage pipeline, ensuring that all decisions satisfy shared system constraints. The framework begins with a mixed-integer linear program (MILP) that selects gateway locations to guarantee full sensor coverage while enforcing segment-level load balancing across the bridge structure. An integer linear program (ILP) then determines the node-to-gateway assignment by minimizing total communication distance subject to coverage feasibility and load-balancing regularization constraints. Each node’s spreading factor and transmit power are jointly optimized through a continuous per-node optimization that balances transmission energy against link margin requirements, replacing conventional adaptive data rate (ADR) and adaptive power control (APC) heuristics with a model-driven approach. Finally, a slot-indexed ILP constructs a feasible uplink schedule that respects spatial-division multiplexing (SDM) capacity limits, per-node duty-cycle regulations, and system-wide latency requirements inherited from the reporting interval. Evaluation on a realistic long-span bridge deployment with 110 LoRa-enabled sensing nodes under KR920 regulations shows that the proposed approach reduces average uplink waiting time by up to 85%, and lowers node duty cycle and per-node energy consumption by up to 75% and 80%, respectively. The framework also achieves near-uniform gateway load distribution across different deployment budgets.

title: Forward-Error-Correction Dynamic Network for High-Accuracy Transmission in Disturbed Multimode Fiber_supp1-3680462.pdf
authors: Sha Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Lasers propagating through scattering media like multimode fibers (MMF) form distorted speckle patterns via multipath scattering, hindering signal recovery. Though data-driven deep learning has potential, neural networks cannot achieve 100% accuracy in noisy dynamic scattering media. This is due to irreversible physical information loss, incomplete training data for time-varying channels, and models lacking physical priors. To solve this, we propose a Forward-Error-Correction Dynamic Network (FECDN), fusing deep residual networks (ResNet) with Reed-Solomon (RS) coding. Guided by forward error correction in information theory, this framework leverages RS coding, a typical information-theoretic tool, to introduce redundancy at the transmitter and achieve real-time byte-level error correction at the receiver, fundamentally protecting the self-supervised training of the neural network from corrupted pseudo-labels, while ResNet captures transmission pattern changes with fewer samples to boost efficiency. Under a 100 m MMF with manual perturbations and without temperature control, the channel exhibits severe instability. In this scenario, the long-term structural similarity index (SSIM) dropped below 0.1 and the short-term SSIM dropped below 0.9. Despite these challenging conditions, FECDN achieved real-time transmission of H.265-compressed 200 × 200 video with zero frame loss and 100% symbol accuracy. This framework addresses instability and accumulation in dynamic scattering channels, providing a robust solution for real-time optical communication and laying the groundwork for intelligent, adaptive optical systems in complex or long-distance scenarios.

title: Retraction Notice: Enhanced hybrid quality prediction model for 8K UHD H.265 video using ANFIS
authors: A. Dhanalakshmi, G. Nagarajan
publish: 2017 4th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Enhanced video coding standard: H.265/ High Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC) elaborated by using SHVC reference software model (SHM), has an aim to develop performance above the current H.265 standard. This paper represents a Quality of Experience (QoE) and Quality of service (QoS) estimate model in a video streaming from the origin of network visual application which is developed in recent years and also 8K video streaming illustration based on the approaching standard of SHVC (Scalable High efficiency Video Coding). SHVC decoder is utilized below the monitor to decode moreover demonstrate in real time the expected SHVC layers. Conversely, ANFIS technique is carried out to interface the Quality of Experience and Quality of service and real time streaming is approved in an unfeasible model which entails an original video sequence. The fundamental objective of this paper is to represent a wireless domain considering 8K UHD H. 265-coded video of the hybrid estimate model of excellent quality. The main contribution of this paper are split up as the examination of the quality impact of the service parameter on the video transmission of 8K UHD H.265-coded and the objective based upon development of quality of visualization by QoS parameters using ANFIS (Adaptive neuro fuzzy interference system). The results showed that the prediction of accurateness is obtained better using an enhanced hybrid model in the series of 50 percentage bit rate reduction and Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR) for the same quality of video, reliability of QoE and 8KUHD video streaming using QoS.

title: A System to Detect Unconscious Bias Using Virtual Patients: System Specifications
authors: Md. Nasfikur R Khan, Sean Walker
publish: 2025 IEEE Integrated STEM Education Conference (ISEC)
abstract: Unconscious bias training fosters diversity, safety, and respect within the workplace by assisting individuals in recognizing, managing, and reducing their biases. Recently, simulation-based training has become increasingly popular among healthcare professionals due to its effectiveness in replicating real-world scenarios. This approach allows for the opportunity to make mistakes, learn, reflect, receive feedback, and improve clinical skills while maintaining patient safety. Nevertheless, immersive technology simulations have certain limitations, including latency issues and challenges related to spatial awareness. This research outlines the system specifications and use cases, accompanied by an activity diagram and data analysis process, while identifying the primary users for implementing virtual patients to trigger unconscious biases in future physicians. This document constitutes the third part of a five-paper series focused on developing a system to trigger unconscious biases in medical students through interactions with virtual patients.

title: Retraction Notice: RTIDS: A Robust Transformer-based Approach for Intrusion Detection System
authors: N Saranya, T Abbinavu, P Arun Kumar, R Gnanasekar, P Guru Prasanth, R Subha
publish: 2024 10th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Intrusion Detection Systems (FIDSs) with foamy disturbance are crucial for preventing security breaches in the constantly growing world of the internet's interconnectivity across many domains. Among the many applications are supply chains, manufacturing, smart homes, smart grids, healthcare, and environmental tracking. Traditional intrusion detection methods, while effective in conventional contexts, require adaptation and enhancement to cater to the unique challenges, including devices with limited resources, memory, and battery capacity, as well as particular protocol stacks. In this study, we develop a lightweight attack detection method that detects and stops attackers from injecting superfluous data into the network using a supervised machine learning-based FIDS. Our simulation results demonstrate that the suggested FIDS- based classifier works well in terms of classification accuracy and detection speed when it is enhanced by a combination of two or three complex features.

title: A Hybrid Framework for Automated Thai Invoice Digitization Integrating YOLO11 and Google Vision OCR
authors: Paphawara Archewavanit, Supakpong Jinarat
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Processing financial documents, particularly invoices, presents a significant challenge in business automation due to the semi-structured nature of document layouts. This research proposes a comprehensive framework for converting Thai language invoices into structured JSON format. The system is built upon the Django web framework and integrates a two-stage object detection pipeline using YOLO11 trained on the Roboflow platform. The first stage partitions the document into semantic regions, while the second stage performs granular table structure analysis. Text extraction is handled via Google Vision OCR, augmented by a custom post-processing logic involving Regular Expressions and Geocoding API validation. Experimental results on a dataset of 30 Thai invoices indicate that the proposed system achieves an accuracy of 83.48 % with an average processing time of 6.22 seconds per document. When compared to manual data entry, the system is approximately 42 times faster. Furthermore, benchmarks against state-of-the-art Large Language Models (Gemini 2.5 Flash) reveal that while the AI model offers slightly higher accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(89.40 \%)$</tex>, the proposed YOLO-based approach is 2.6 times faster, making it more suitable for real-time applications. The study also highlights the limitations of current open-source OCR tools for the Thai language.

title: A Study on the Content Analysis of Promotion Skincare Product Through Short Videos on Chinese Market Platforms
authors: Shuhan Yang, Danaitun Pongpatcharatrontep
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: The study employs the AISAS model (Attention-Interest-Search-Action-Share) to analyze 100 Douyin short videos posted by ten small and medium-sized skincare brands from October 2024 to October 2025. Video text and interaction data were extracted through manual annotation and AI-based semantic recognition, and descriptive statistics were used to identify content characteristics across the five stages. The results reveal a “front-loaded” content pattern, with the highest proportions appearing in the Attention (28 %) and Action (26 %) stages, while the Interest, Search, and Share stages are notably underrepresented. This imbalance leads to weak interest cultivation, limited trust-building, and a disrupted pathway for word-of-mouth diffusion. The findings suggest that small and medium-sized brands should strengthen informational and share-oriented content to complete the AISAS cycle and shift from short-term conversion toward long-term brand development.

title: Symbolic Modeling for Multistage Parametric Amplifiers in the Frequency Domain
authors: Yuriy Shapovalov, Dariya Bachyk, Roman Romaniuk, Vadym Minziuk
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Modeling complex linear periodically time-varying (LPTV) circuits in the frequency domain remains a relevant challenge in both science and engineering. Symbolic circuit-analysis methods are effective for multivariate modeling. The Advanced Frequency Symbolic Method (AFS-method) has been implemented in the system UDF MAOPCs (User Defined Functions for Multivariate Analysis and Optimization of Parametric Circuits). In this work, the AFS-method is applied to perform multivariate frequency analysis of multistage LPTV amplifiers. Transfer functions from the input current to the output voltage of the multistage LPTV amplifiers are derived, and output signal spectra are obtained. For validation, the same amplifiers are analyzed using Micro-Cap 12. The results show a strong agreement between the two methods, with symbolic modeling in the system UDF MAOPCs requiring approximately half the computation time of numerical modeling in Micro Cap 12. These findings confirm the effectiveness of the AFS-method for frequency analysis of LPTV circuits and its applicability to multistage LPTV amplifier modeling.

title: A SIRA-CTMC Model for Analyzing and Predicting Cybersecurity Threats
authors: Serhiy Semenyuk, Ihor Zhuravel
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Malware remains a critical cybersecurity challenge, exploiting network vulnerabilities to propagate rapidly and inflict widespread damage. The Conficker worm, known for its advanced propagation techniques, is a prime example of such a complex threat. However, the threat landscape continues to evolve beyond complex propagation logic. Modern malware increasingly leverages Machine Learning (ML) techniques to evade detection. For instance, attackers utilize neural networks to obfuscate code, while defenders have begun employing Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to detect malware by converting binary files into image representations for classification. While this paper focuses on the propagation dynamics of worms like Conficker, understanding these advanced evasion and detection mechanisms highlights the necessity for models that can accurately predict outbreaks even amidst stochastic obfuscation. Traditional deterministic models, adapted from biological epidemic theory, often fail to capture the true nature of real-world propagation. We propose a stochastic SIRA (Susceptible-Infected-Recovered-Antidotal) model formulated as a Continuous-Time Markov Chain (CTMC) to address these limitations. This model extends the classical SIR model with the 'Antidotal' state, which represents hosts that are patched or vaccinated against the threat. We move from deterministic differential equations to a stochastic, event-driven process by using a CTMC. Such approach models demographic stochasticity, the inherent randomness in individual host-to-host interactions. The model is defined by discrete events (infection, recovery, vaccination) occurring at specific rates, what captures the chance-driven nature of propagation. We simulate the model's behavior using the Gillespie Algorithm (also known as the Stochastic Simulation Algorithm) on historical data from the Conficker worm. This method generates exact stochastic paths, allowing for a more realistic representation of malware dynamics, including the probability of outbreak extinction. To fit the model to observed data, we propose using simulation-based inference methods, such as Approximate Bayesian Computation (ABC), to estimate the key event rates (infection, recovery, and antidotal). This approach demonstrates how a CTMC-based SIRA model offers a more granular representation of malware dynamics, which is crucial for developing proactive cybersecurity defenses.

title: Study of Neural Network Performance for Enhancing SDN Resilience Against Cyberattacks
authors: Vitalii Savchenko, Pavlo Shchypanskyi, Oleksandr Matsko, Serhii Lienkov
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Software-Defined Networks (SDN) provide flexible and programmable infrastructures but remain vulnerable to targeted cyberattacks due to their centralized architecture. This study investigates the performance of neural network-based approaches for enhancing SDN resilience under dynamic cyber threats. Three architectures – simple Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) – were trained on a dataset of 1,000 network parameter realizations and tested on a control set of 200 examples with parameters computed using optimization methods. Results demonstrate that RNN and LSTM effectively capture temporal dependencies, achieving lower prediction errors (RNN: 2.2%, LSTM: 1.4%) compared to MLP (4.9%). LSTM provides superior accuracy for long sequences, while simple RNN offers a favorable trade-off between performance and complexity. These findings highlight the potential of recurrent architectures for adaptive SDN management and suggest avenues for integrating neural networks with control strategies to maintain network functionality under cyberattack conditions.

title: Machine Learning-Based Optimization of 5G Network Topology
authors: Serhii Siden, Roman Tsarov, Svitlana Kiiko, Andrii Pavlov, Mykhailo Kutovyi
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The paper considers the problem of 5G network topology optimization in an urban environment. A mathematical model and a solution algorithm for this problem are proposed, utilizing machine learning methods. The proposed model enables the determination of the optimal number of base stations and their placement while maximizing the throughput of network users. The model is developed for Urban Macro (UMa) signal propagation conditions and accounts for intra-system interference and constraints on the number of connections to a single base station. Simulation modeling was conducted to verify the efficiency of the proposed approach.

title: Class E Oscillator Stabilized by a Piezoceramic Resonator
authors: Vladimir Krizhanovski, Dmitrii Chernov, Vasyl Komarov, Andrei Grebennikov
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The paper investigates a novel design of a Class E high-frequency oscillator, in which a piezoceramic resonator is proposed for enhancing frequency stability. Piezoelectric resonators are known to have a safe drive level limitation. To resolve this contradiction, it is suggested to create two feedback branches to ensure sufficient control power at the Class E oscillator's transistor and to limit the resonator's drive level to safe values. An oscillator design operating at a frequency of approximately 3.6 MHz has been developed, and its experimental study has been conducted to demonstrate the proposed circuit's operation.

title: Using Correlation Analysis to Detect Excessive Distortion in Audio-Frequency Track Circuit Signals
authors: Volodymyr Havryliuk, Serhij Shemanov
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Audio-frequency track circuits are critical devices that directly impact train movement safety. They are used to determine the condition of a monitored track section (free or occupied). Audio-frequency track circuits operate by transmitting a signal along the rails from a generator to a receiver, and the receiver decodes the incoming signal to determine the condition of the monitored track section. Rails are an imperfect signal transmission channel, the parameters of which vary significantly depending on external conditions (temperature and humidity), component aging, contacts deterioration due to corrosion or vibration, and other factors. This leads to significant fluctuations in the signal parameters arriving at the receiver input. Furthermore, the signal is susceptible to strong electromagnetic interference from rolling stock. Excessive signal distortion in track circuits can cause incorrect receiver responses, leading to train delays or even emergency situations. This paper investigates the application of correlation analysis methods in both the time and frequency domains for the timely detection of excessive signal distortion in audio-frequency track circuits. The results demonstrate that the cross-correlation coefficient and the coherence function can be effectively used as diagnostic parameters for monitoring the technical condition of audio-frequency track circuits. The implementation of these correlation-based diagnostic methods in railway monitoring systems can enhance the reliability of track circuits and support timely maintenance to prevent potential failures.

title: CVLS-ST: A Cross-View Long-Short Framework for Traffic Risk Prediction in Intelligent Urban Transportation Systems
authors: Yachao Yuan, Xingyu Chen, Zixiang Peng, Muting Li, Zhen Yu, Yingwen Wu, Thar Baker
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: As a cornerstone of smart city consumer ecosystems, accurate traffic accident risk prediction is vital for proactive safety services in intelligent transportation systems. However, current solutions often fail to integrate heterogeneous relational features (e.g., historical risk correlations) or effectively balance long-term periodic patterns with transient dynamics. To address these issues, we introduce a Cross-View Long-Short Spatial-Temporal Framework (CVLS-ST) for traffic risk prediction, which innovatively decouples long- and short-term dependencies to achieve more precise urban traffic risk prediction. Specifically, we design a cross-view attention graph network to effectively capture complex and diverse interactions across multiple relational graphs. Besides, we develop a Mamba block that incorporates state-space modeling into LSTM’s gating architecture to effectively capture global spatial semantics and temporal dependencies. Moreover, we introduce a state-inherited strategy to jointly and accurately model the long-term trends and short-term dynamics of traffic risk. Extensive experiments on two real-world datasets show that the F1-score of CVLS-ST outperforms the state-of-the-art by 20.37% on CHI and 5.83% on NYC, validating its effectiveness in complex urban environments.

title: iSeg: An Iterative Refinement-based Framework for Training-free Segmentation
authors: Lin Sun, Jiale Cao, Jin Xie, Fahad Shahbaz Khan, Yanwei Pang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Stable Diffusion has demonstrated strong image synthesis ability to given text descriptions, suggesting it to contain strong semantic clue for grouping objects. The researchers have explored employing Stable Diffusion for training-free segmentation. Most existing approaches refine cross-attention map by self-attention map once, demonstrating that self-attention map contains useful semantic information to improve segmentation. To fully utilize self-attention map, we present a deep experimental analysis on iteratively refining cross-attention map with self-attention map, and propose an effective iterative refinement framework for training-free segmentation, named iSeg. Our iSeg introduces an entropy-reduced self-attention module that utilizes a gradient descent scheme to reduce the entropy of self-attention map, thereby suppressing the weak responses corresponding to irrelevant global information. Leveraging the entropy-reduced self-attention module, our iSeg stably improves cross-attention map with iterative refinement. Further, we design a category-enhanced cross-attention module to generate accurate cross-attention map, providing a better initial input for iterative refinement. Extensive experiments across different datasets and diverse segmentation tasks (weakly-supervised semantic segmentation, open-vocabulary semantic segmentation, unsupervised segmentation, and mask generation on synthetic dataset) reveal the merits of proposed contributions, leading to promising performance. For unsupervised semantic segmentation on Cityscapes, our iSeg achieves an absolute gain of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.8\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> in terms of mIoU compared to the best existing training-free approach in literature. Moreover, our proposed iSeg can support segmentation with different kinds of images and interactions, and also be used as a post-processing, or in different frameworks, to improve training-free segmentation. The project is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://linsun449.github.io/iSeg</uri>.

title: MaliVD: Source Code Vulnerability Localization via Attention-Based Multi-Modal Learning
authors: Enze Dai, Shuo Wang, Xi Xiao, Qing Li, Sheng Wen, Tianqing Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Source code vulnerabilities represent a critical threat to software security, potentially leading to severe consequences such as data breaches and system failures. Traditional static analysis tools, while widely used, suffer from high false positive rates and struggle to adapt to the increasing complexity of modern software. Deep learning-based approaches hold promise for automated vulnerability detection, but they face challenges including limited dataset quality, inadequate feature extraction, and lack of precise vulnerability localization capabilities. To address these limitations, we propose MaliVD, a novel vulnerability detection method in source code that leverages a multi-modal attention mechanism. MaliVD not only identifies vulnerability types but also pinpoints the specific lines of code where vulnerabilities are triggered. The model extracts sequential, tree-based, and graph-based features from source code and employs specialized neural networks to learn these diverse representations. By strategically focusing on Points of Interest within the code, MaliVD effectively prioritizes potentially vulnerable code regions, enhancing both detection accuracy and localization precision. Experimental results show that when compared with eight advanced vulnerability detection models across three large datasets, MaliVD demonstrates superior vulnerability detection and localization capabilities, maintaining high F1 scores and localization precision. Particularly on the ReliVul dataset, the F1 score is improved by 21.82%, and the Top-5 localization accuracy is 18% higher than other methods, with lower false positives across six mainstream vulnerability types, validating MaliVD’s practical application value in real-world environments.

title: Retraction Notice: IoT System Accomplishment using BlockChain in Validating and Data Security with Cloud
authors: J. Kingsleen Solomon Doss, S. Kamalakkannan
publish: 2020 Fourth International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: In a block channel IoT system, sensitive details can be leaked by means of the proof of work or address check, as data or application Validation data is applied on the blockchain. In this, the zero-knowledge evidence is applied to a smart metering system to show how to improve the anonymity of the blockchain for privacy safety without disclosing information as a public key. Within this article, a blockchain has been implemented to deter security risks such as data counterfeiting by utilizing intelligent meters. Zero-Knowledge Proof, an anonymity blockchain technology, has been implemented through block inquiry to prevent threats to security like personal information infringement. It was suggested that intelligent contracts would be used to avoid falsification of intelligent meter data and abuse of personal details.

title: Retraction Notice: Hash Tag-Based Sentiment Analysis Using A Deep Learning Approach
authors: R. Venkatesan, M. Sangeetha, S. Nivedha, M. Selvakumar, K.S. Chandru, P. Parthasarathi

abstract: Social commerce uses a variety of social networking sites like Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, Pinterest, and business Whatsapp as a platform to promote products and services. Since advertising, a product, or service in s-comm will reach as high as a possible number of users on networking sites. Ads will spread virally by sharing among friends and friends of friends. So that wider reachability is possible and shortly will get the customer for our business. The success of an s-comm campaign is measured by the degree to which consumers interact with the sellers through review comments, likes, and shares between friends. Customer behavior analysis is needed for s-comm marketing because social commerce can significantly boost economic growth. The sentiment analysis of customer recommendations was investigated using a dataset of women's s-comm apparel hashtags and reviews. So for such reviews are experimented with different machine learning algorithms (Decision tree, SVM, Random Forest, KNN, Naïve Bayes and LSTM). These models handled the common English reviews and compared the metrics (Accuracy, Sensitivity and Specificity) with one another. Instead of working with individual model, this paper introduces a novel hybrid approach that integrates CNN and Ada Boost LSTM algorithms to provide better results in sentiment analysis when compared to other machine learning algorithms mentioned above. While considering the hash tag with common review comments the hybrid system is out performed. The comparison analysis is done for performance matrices namely accuracy of 98%, specificity of 98.1%, and sensitivity of 98.1%. In the s-commerce transaction industry, this hybrid approach will help to create a deeper understanding of consumer sentiment analysis and psychologically attract clients.

title: Blind Radio Map Construction via Topology Guided Manifold Learning_supp1-3680610.pdf
authors: Edith Ngai
publish: N/A
abstract: Constructing radio maps traditionally requires extensive site surveys with precise location labels, resulting in costly and time-consuming calibration. Conventional approaches derive labels from inertial measurement units (IMUs), but are constrained by device-level access permissions and the need for pre-installed IMU hardware. In this paper, we present a calibration-free radio map construction method that relies solely on Channel State Information (CSI) measurements, thereby obviating the need for location labels. Our key insight is to embed CSI data into a two-dimensional geographic space using a neural network, without any label information. The primary challenge is to preserve the real-world topology in the embedded space. To address this issue, we propose a novel Topology-Guided Manifold Learning (TGML) approach that learns a low-dimensional embedding through self-supervision based on its mapping to a topological map in the geographic space. Specifically, we introduce an embedding network that learns locally smoothed proximity, thus creating a compact low-dimensional representation in the latent space. We further employ a regularized transport method with a differentiable Sinkhorn distance to establish an optimal mapping between the latent and geographic spaces. We use the resulting mapping deviation to supervise the training of the embedding network, enabling continuous refinement through self-supervision. Experiments in an office environment show that TGML achieves an average localization error of 2.37m and a relative beam estimation error of 6.68%, outperforming state-of-the-art methods.

title: Photonic Structuration of the Airy-Bessel Beam for High-Performance Fiber-Optic Refractive Index Sensing
authors: Bodem Indraja, Arijit datta, Sreenivasulu tupakula, Swagata Samanta, Saswat kumar ram
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The emergence of structured light as a controllable photonic entity has transformed the manipulation of electromagnetic fields, facilitating advanced light-matter interactions that critically enhance the operational efficiency of diverse fiber-optic sensors. Within this emergent framework, the Airy-Bessel beam, characterized by its non-diffracting propagation and self-healing dynamics, stands as a paradigmatically superior candidate for fiber-based refractive-index measurements. In this investigation, we employed the Airy-Bessel excitation within a decladded multimode fiber to transcend the inherent constraints of conventional Gaussian illumination, whose efficacy is typically curtailed by shallow evanescent-field penetration and diminished mode-medium interaction. By tailoring the excitation conditions, higher-order guided modes are efficiently stimulated, thereby amplifying the evanescent-wave contribution and enabling a superior refractive index sensitivity. The proposed sensor architecture was validated using full-vector beam propagation method (BPM) numerical modeling performed in OptiBPM (v13.1.3, Optiwave Inc.), allowing a comprehensive analysis of Airy-Bessel beam evolution within the sensing structure. The sensor demonstrates absolute sensitivities of 1502.96 dB/RIU and 1878.72 dB/RIU for sensing lengths of 20 cm and 25 cm, respectively, with corresponding detection resolutions of 6.65×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup> RIU and 5.32×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup> RIU. These results affirm a notable advancement over Gaussian beam configurations and highlight that structured-beam excitation enables scalable sensing without the need for geometric alterations such as tapering, side-polishing, U-bending, surface-functionalized designs, etc. Therefore, the proposed sensing method furnishes a potent pathway for futuristic fiber-optic sensing across chemical, biomedical, and environmental domains where ultralow refractive index detection is imperative.

title: Lightweight Edge Intelligence: Adapting 1.5B LLM via LoRA for Log Analysis in Campus Networks
authors: Zhenpeng Li
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The practical adoption of Large Language Models (LLMs) in cybersecurity remains constrained by computational cost, deployment complexity, and increasingly strict data-privacy requirements. These challenges are particularly acute in resource-limited environments such as vocational colleges and similar resource-constrained institutional environments, where cloud-based security analytics may be undesirable or infeasible, while traditional rule-based intrusion detection systems often struggle with noisy alerts and limited semantic understanding of network events. To address this gap, I propose Edge-SecGPT, an on-premise framework for lightweight log screening under constrained campus deployment conditions. Built on the Qwen2.5-1.5B backbone, Edge-SecGPT adopts parameter-efficient adaptation through Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) together with 4-bit NF4 quantization. Rather than targeting maximal model scale, the framework is designed for deployment on resource-constrained single-GPU edge-oriented hardware and operates as a task-restricted binary log-screening module for privacy-conscious campus-network analysis. To improve methodological rigor, I evaluate the framework using a stricter temporal file-holdout protocol, where one earlier log window is used for training, a subsequent window is used for validation, and a later unseen temporal holdout window is reserved for final evaluation. Across 32,762 desensitized campus log records, the revised study frames Edge-SecGPT as a localized lightweight LoRA-based log-screening framework together with a controlled serialization study under the stricter temporal file-holdout protocol and an additional same-protocol conventional baseline. On the later unseen temporal holdout, the conventional logistic-regression baseline achieved 77.96% precision, 98.48% recall, and 87.03% F1-score, with an estimated 826.5 false alarms/day. Under the same temporal split, the TinyLlama-1.1B LoRA baseline achieved 94.37% precision, 100.00% recall, and 97.10% F1-score, while the raw-JSON Qwen2.5-1.5B configuration achieved the strongest empirical performance among the compared configurations under the present single-site temporal-holdout protocol and current label-construction regime (94.68% precision, 100.00% recall, and 97.27% F1-score). The corresponding structured configuration achieved 93.81% precision, 99.18% recall, and 96.42% F1-score. These results suggest that serialization choice materially affects deployment behavior in the present within-site setting, while broader attack-semantic generalization remains to be validated.

title: Compact Silicon-Photonics Waveguide- Confined Receiving and Transmitting Antenna with Signal Remodulation for Indoor Optical Wireless Communication
authors: Yin-He Jian, Mikołaj Wolny, Chi-Wai Chow, Eduward Tangdiongga
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: We propose and demonstrate a compact silicon photonics (SiPh)-based waveguide (WG)-confined receiving antenna (RA) and transmitting antenna (TA) integrated with an enhanced direct intensity remodulation scheme. In a narrow-beam-steered system, the RA exhibits only 5.3-dB power variation over a 12° angular range, supporting data rates > 28.12 Gbps, while in an ultra-narrow-beam-steered system, a 6-dB field-of-view (FoV) of 6.4° enables data rates > 70 Gbps. The combined RA-TA architecture supports bidirectional channels without additional UL lasers. The proposed signal interference cancellation (SIC) algorithm further enhances UL performance. Experimental results show signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) improvements up to 2.37 dB and potential UL throughput gains up to 45% for higher DL modulation depths.

title: Modality-Mix Learning: Promoting Multimodal Learning Through Multilabel Objective_supp1-3677806.pdf
authors: Nannan Lu
publish: N/A
abstract: Multimodal fusion provides a comprehensive way to understand the world by integrating data from different sources. However, some studies believe that due to the optimization imbalance, partial modalities cannot be fully learned during multimodal learning. They attempt to achieve the balance between different modalities by controlling their learning process but ignore the function of the learning objective as an essential factor. The uniform objective for all modalities leads to the network being unable to sufficiently exploit discriminative information from different modalities. Therefore, we propose a new multimodal learning method, namely, modality-mix learning (MM learning), aiming to promote the sufficient learning of each modality via the designed multilabel objective. MM learning generates modality-mixed samples by combining modalities of different samples with varied labels, transforming the single label of a sample into a probability vector representing multilabel information. These modality-mixed samples are then fed into the network, which is trained to recognize the varying proportions of multilabel information. In addition, we introduce a bilevel learning scheme, where the network is first trained using standard learning to capture general features and select samples with strong prediction, followed by MM learning on these selected samples to further optimize the subexploration modality. MM learning forces different objective information to be learned from different modalities, avoiding the insufficient learning of modalities caused by the uniform learning objective. The experimental results show that the method can significantly boost different fusion strategies and methods in diversified multimodal datasets and improve the robustness of multimodal networks as well.

title: Joint Optimization in Pinching-Antenna-Assisted NOMA-MEC Systems via DRL
authors: Yujie Ma, Wenqi Huang, Zhiguo Ding, Daniel K. C. So, Vimal Bhatia
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: This work investigates the integration of pinching antennas into latency-sensitive mobile edge computing (MEC) systems, where a hybrid non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) strategy is employed for task offloading to minimize energy consumption. An energy minimization problem is formulated to jointly optimize the pinching antenna position, transmit power, and time allocation. Due to the non-convexity caused by highly coupled optimization variables, an entropy-based deep reinforcement learning (DRL) approach is developed. Simulation results show that the proposed soft actor-critic (SAC) framework achieves near-optimal performance under dynamic channel conditions while maintaining stability and generalization. Meanwhile, with the integration of pinching antennas, hybrid NOMA outperforms other strategies in latency-critical scenarios.

title: Design of a Low-Profile Wideband Horizontally Polarized Omnidirectional-like Loop Antenna with Characteristic Mode Analysis
authors: Dazhi Piao, Juan Wang, Meiwei Zhang, Jiaojiao Song
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A low-profile wideband horizontally polarized (HP) omnidirectional-like loop antenna is proposed based on characteristic mode analysis (CMA). By analyzing the current distribution on a metal loop using CMA, the mode J<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub> is observed to exhibit a HP radiation pattern. To effectively excite mode J<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub>, nonperiodic and nonuniform slots are introduced into the loop structure to achieve capacitive loading, which enhances both the modal significance and the modal bandwidth. In order to further extend the bandwidth, a dual-loop structure is formulated by loading a parasitic loop. The proposed single-loop and dual-loop antennas are fabricated, and the measurement results show that -10 dB impedance bandwidths of 67.5% (2.75-5.55 GHz) and 88.7% (2.7-7.0 GHz) can be realized, respectively. The physical dimensions of the antennas are π × 28<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>× 0.8 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> (π × (0.26λ°)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> × 0.007λ°) for the single-loop and π × 30<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>× 0.8 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> (π × (0.27λ°)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> × 0.007λ°) for the dual-loop design, where λ₀ denotes the free space wavelength at the lowest operating frequency. Furthermore, the relative bandwidth-to-area ratios of the two antennas reach 318 and 387%/λ°², respectively, which are significantly higher than those of the previously reported HP omnidirectional antennas.

title: A DQN-Guided Genetic Algorithm With Local Intensification for Agile Earth Observation Satellite Scheduling
authors: Jiang Zhu, Qingpei Hu, Jian Wu, Yonghao Du, Xiaolu Liu, Yanjie Song, Witold Pedrycz, Lining Xing
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: The agile earth observation satellite scheduling problem with time-dependent transition constraints is a complex combinatorial optimization problem that has been proven NP-hard. To address this challenge, a Deep Q-network guided genetic algorithm with a local intensification strategy (DQN-LIGA) is proposed. First, we adopt a hybrid initialization method that combines greedy and random strategies to generate a high-quality and diverse initial population. Next, multiple crossover–mutation operator pairs are designed. A Deep Q-network is then employed as a policy model to adaptively select operator combinations based on individual and evolutionary states. Moreover, a simulated physical oscillation strategy (SPOS) is proposed to periodically perturb elite individuals, which is designed to refine solutions and strengthen local intensification throughout the evolutionary process. Finally, extensive experiments are conducted on 20 benchmark instances under both China and World scenarios. The results demonstrate that DQN-LIGA consistently achieves higher observation profits compared to state-of-the-art algorithms, and the integration of SPOS further improves solution quality, especially under complex scheduling conditions.

title: Evolutionary Algorithm for Determining the Data Transmission Route Between Sensor Nodes
authors: Yaroslav Pyrih, Yuliia Pyrih, Taras Andrukhiv
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: A genetic algorithm is presented, the purpose of which is to minimise the weight of the route between sensor nodes. The total weight of the edges, which reflects the distance or delay value when transmitting data between nodes, is used as the optimisation criterion. The proposed approach involves representing the route as an individual and using genetic operators to form new generations. The fitness of individuals is assessed based on a function inversely proportional to the total weight of the route, which allows one to approach the optimal path with minimal costs. To study the operation of the presented algorithm, simulation modelling was carried out using the developed software product. Based on the results obtained, it is shown that the author's algorithm allowed determining the shortest route in terms of both distance and delay value, compared to the greedy one.

title: Enhancing Research Administration Services in Universities: Research Grant Disbursement Online Tracking System
authors: Shisa Singkhamfu
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Higher education institutions encounter intricate and swiftly evolving obstacles, especially regarding research administration, a fundamental task influencing university rankings and academic reputation. This paper delineates a case study from the College of Arts, Media, and Technology (CAMT) of Chiang Mai University (CMU) regarding the development of an “Research Grant Disbursement Online Tracking System”. The research examines the integration of Lean Management concepts with Knowledge Management (KM) principles to transform tacit information into explicit and accessible knowledge. By delineating explicit six-step workflows, the system mitigates “administrative friction” and operational waste. Results indicate significant organizational impact in terms of time savings and improved user satisfaction.

title: Cebuano Speech Tutor Using Hybrid TDNN-HMM Phoneme and Prosodic Feature Recognition
authors: Christian Gabriel Ibaoc, Antonio Luiz Veloso, Nigel Kristoffer Valero, Shawne Michael Tumalad, Ronald Pascual
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Speech processing technology is a growing technology that relies on the effective use of available speech data. In the Philippines, most speech processing, from recognition to synthesis, is focused on either Tagalog or English, with Cebuano being often neglected. This study focuses on the Cebuano language and the prospect of developing a computer-assisted speech tutor using a TDNN-HMM hybrid speech model-based phoneme recognition, to teach language learners up to Level A1 CEFR in the Cebuano language. The model was evaluated using adapted WER and SER metrics as well as expert validation, and user improvement was evaluated through pre-tests, post-tests, model-based data, and expert validation. The speech model was calculated to have 2.64 percent WER and 8.00 percent SER, though expert assessments, on average, determined it to have a 17.82 percent error rate. Meanwhile, users of the website were found to have improved both their vocabulary and pronunciation, with an average score of 6.43 to 12.78 out of 15 from pre-test to post-test, and an increasing trend line of correct syllables per day with a strong coefficient of determination. User improvement was validated by an expert with 20 samples of final audio recordings. Recommendations for future research are discussed.

title: Effective English Communication for Food and Beverage Staff to Achieve Guest Satisfaction
authors: Aye Mon, Premchit Khurana
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: English communication is vital in the hospitality industry, especially for food and beverage (F&B) staff interacting with international guests. This study examines the challenges of English communication among food and beverage (F&B) staff in Thailand, its impact on guest satisfaction, and strategies for improvement. Data was collected through interviews as well as secondary research. The study highlights how language barriers, limited vocabulary, and cultural differences affect service quality and presents recommendations for enhancing staff English proficiency for better guest experiences.

title: A Logistic Regression Pipeline for Chiang Mai Air Quality Risk using Scaling, SMOTE, and Splines
authors: Sane Yu, Nina Bencheva
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Accuracy of air quality risk prediction in Chiang Mai is a significant challenge. There are acute <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{PM}_{2.5}$</tex> pollution in Chiang Mai, Thailand in every year from February to May. The interpretable model like standard logistic regression is beneficial for operational public health sectors, but the model performance is weakened by complex real-world data and extreme class imbalance. This study investigates how pipeline design specifically features scaling, nonlinear spline transformations, and the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) can materially improve logistic regression for the prediction of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{PM}_{2.5}$</tex> in Chiang Mai. This study uses a public dataset of 29,078 hourly meteorological and pollution records for Chiang Mai with a severe class imbalance (approximately 3 percent high risk). This study evaluates four distinct pipelines against a deep learning benchmark. Results demonstrate that standard logistic regression fails to detect high risk events, yielding zero recall. However, the introduction of SMOTE successfully restores operational utility, increasing recall to over 0.81. The combined pipeline, integrating both SMOTE and cubic spline feature engineering, provides the best overall discrimination among all interpretable models. It achieves a Receiver Operating Characteristic Area Under the Curve (ROC AUC) of 0.823, balancing the need for high sensitivity in disaster warning systems with the requirement for model transparency. While the deep learning benchmark sets a higher performance ceiling, the results demonstrate that for highly imbalanced data, an engineered logistic regression pipeline offers the optimal balance of safety and auditability for municipal environmental monitoring.

title: Ethical AIGC Pipelines for Film and Animation Production
authors: Wai Yie Leong
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence-Generated Content (AIGC) has rapidly reshaped film and animation production by enabling automated asset creation, accelerated ideation, and complex multimodal storytelling. Despite these advancements, the integration of AIGC technologies introduces substantial ethical risks, including biased character representation, culturally insensitive outputs, unclear authorship, dataset opacity, copyright infringement, and weakened creative labour protection. This paper develops a comprehensive ethical framework for AIGC pipelines tailored to cinematic and animation workflows. Through an extensive review of current generative models-diffusion systems, multimodal transformers, neural animation generators, and large language models-the study identifies recurring ethical deficiencies that undermine responsible deployment. A technical methodology is proposed, featuring transparent dataset provenance tracking, embedded fairness auditing, cultural-sensitivity validators, copyright-risk scoring, and human-in-the-loop approval mechanisms as integral components of the AIGC pipeline. Experimental simulations across storyboard generation, character design, motion synthesis, and dialogue creation demonstrate that the Ethical AIGC Pipeline (E-AIGCP) significantly reduces bias propagation, minimizes copyright exposure, and enhances human-AI creative alignment. Results indicate a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 2 \%}$</tex> reduction in high-risk outputs and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 1 \%}$</tex> improvement in perceived creative integrity. The study provides a structured, technically rigorous blueprint for studios and regulators seeking to implement responsible AIGC systems in film and animation production.

title: The Improved Models for Calculation of Human Body SAR in the Case of Sitting Position
authors: Mykhaylo Andriychuk, Taras Nazarovets
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The calculation of Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) is carried out in the framework of the advanced modeling based on the improved model of linear antenna, which takes into account the seated posture of human, and the FDTD approach, based on the calculation of the internal characteristics of the EM field. The numerical data for both the approaches are proposed and issues related to the effectiveness and correctness of method are discussed. The simulation data confirm proximity of results obtained by both the methods.
